title: DAY 1: How the Web Works & Your First HTML Page
objective: By the end of Day 1, you will understand how the web works, have your coding environment set up, and create your first HTML page.

detailedContent.title: The Web Demystified
detailedContent.content:
When you visit a URL, your browser performs a DNS lookup, sends an HTTP request, and receives a response containing HTML, CSS, and JS.

**The Core Three:**
- **HTML:** Structure (The Skeleton).
- **CSS:** Styling (The Paint/Furniture).
- **JavaScript:** Interactivity (The Plumbing/Electricity).

detailedContent.title: Your First Tags
detailedContent.content:
HTML uses tags like `

`, `

`, ``, and ``. Attributes like `href` and `src` provide extra information. Every page starts with a `` and wraps content in ``, ``, and `` tags.

video.title: HTML Crash Course for Absolute Beginners
video.description: Traversy Media provides a comprehensive look at HTML tags, attributes, and page structure.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: Environment Setup (5 minutes)
steps: Install VS Code and create your first 'index.html' file

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Personal Intro Page (15 minutes)
steps: Create a page with an H1 for your name, a paragraph introduction, and a list of 3 hobbies
Include an image and a link to your favorite website

reflectionPrompt: How does it feel to see your own code rendered as a real webpage in a browser?

title: DAY 2: Styling with CSS – Making Things Beautiful
objective: By the end of Day 2, you will be able to style your HTML with colors, fonts, spacing, and layouts.

detailedContent.title: CSS Foundations
detailedContent.content:
CSS selectors (Element, Class, ID) allow you to target specific HTML. Properties like `color`, `font-family`, and `background-color` transform the look.

**The Box Model:** Every element is a box with Content, Padding, Border, and Margin. Understanding the difference between these is the key to mastering layout.

video.title: CSS Crash Course for beginners
video.description: Learn how to use selectors, the box model, and layout properties to design your pages.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: The Styling Challenge (20 minutes)
steps: Create a 'style.css' file and link it to your HTML
Change the font of your headings, and add a background color and padding to your hobby list

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Box Model Experiment (10 minutes)
steps: Add a border to your image and use 'margin: 0 auto' to center a container div

reflectionPrompt: What is the biggest difference you noticed between 'margin' and 'padding' when styling your list?

title: DAY 3: Responsive Design & Flexbox
objective: By the end of Day 3, you will create layouts that adapt to different screen sizes using Flexbox and media queries.

detailedContent.title: Modern Layouts
detailedContent.content:
**Flexbox:** Use `display: flex` on a container to easily align child items. Properties like `justify-content` and `align-items` handle the spacing.

**Responsive Strategy:** Use media queries (`@media`) to change your layout when the screen width gets smaller. Always design mobile-first for the best user experience.

video.title: Learn Flexbox in 15 Minutes
video.description: Web Dev Simplified explains the core concepts of the Flexbox layout model.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: Responsive Navigation (15 minutes)
steps: Build a horizontal nav bar that stacks vertically on mobile screens using a media query

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Card Layout (15 minutes)
steps: Create 3 cards using Flexbox that wrap to new lines as the window shrinks

reflectionPrompt: Why is it important to test your website on a phone as well as a desktop?

title: DAY 4: Introduction to JavaScript – Interactivity
objective: By the end of Day 4, you will be able to add JavaScript to create simple interactive features.

detailedContent.title: Bringing the Page to Life
detailedContent.content:
JavaScript handles behavior. You can select elements using `document.querySelector`, listen for events like `click`, and change content with `.textContent`.

**Variables & Functions:** Store data in `let` or `const`, and group actions into reusable `function` blocks.

video.title: JavaScript Crash Course for Beginners
video.description: An introduction to variables, functions, and manipulating the Document Object Model (DOM).

exercises.title: Exercise 1: Dark Mode Toggle (15 minutes)
steps: Write a script that toggles a 'dark-mode' class on the body when a button is clicked

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Simple Counter (15 minutes)
steps: Create two buttons that increment and decrement a displayed number on the screen

reflectionPrompt: What was the most challenging part of getting your first JavaScript function to run?

title: DAY 5: Working with Forms & User Input
objective: By the end of Day 5, you will be able to handle form submissions and validate user input.

detailedContent.title: Communication with Users
detailedContent.content: Forms are essential for data collection. Use `event.preventDefault()` to stop the page from reloading on submit. You can then validate the values (like checking for an '@' in an email) before 'sending' the data.

video.title: Learn HTML Forms in 25 Minutes
video.description: Covers input types, labels, and basic form validation techniques.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: Contact Form (15 minutes)
steps: Build a form with name, email, and message fields, and style it with CSS

exercises.title: Exercise 2: JavaScript Validation (15 minutes)
steps: Display a success message only if all form fields are filled out correctly

reflectionPrompt: Why is client-side validation important for user experience, even if the server does its own check?

title: DAY 6: Hosting & Going Live
objective: By the end of Day 6, you will deploy your website live for the world to see.

detailedContent.title: Deploying Your Code
detailedContent.content: Your site stays on your computer until you host it. **GitHub Pages** and **Netlify** are popular free options for static sites. Deployment is as simple as pushing your code to a repository or dragging a folder into a dashboard.

video.title: How to Host a Website for Free
video.description: A step-by-step guide to using GitHub Pages and Netlify to share your projects.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: GitHub Pages Setup (20 minutes)
steps: Create a GitHub account, upload your files, and enable GitHub Pages in the settings

exercises.title: Exercise 2: Go Live! (10 minutes)
steps: Share your new live URL with a friend or your prompt journal

reflectionPrompt: How does it feel knowing your site is now accessible to anyone with an internet connection?

title: DAY 7: Final Project – Multi-Page Responsive Site
objective: By the end of Day 7, you will build and deploy a complete multi-page website from scratch.

detailedContent.title: The Culmination
detailedContent.content:
**Final Requirements:**
- 3+ HTML pages linked by a navigation bar.
- Shared external CSS for a consistent look.
- Responsive Flexbox layout.
- At least one interactive JS feature (e.g., Form validation or Dark mode).

video.title: Build a Responsive Website in 1 Hour
video.description: Traversy Media shows you how to bring all the pieces together into a professional project.

exercises.title: Exercise 1: The Master Launch (90+ minutes)
steps: Plan -> Code -> Style -> Interactivity -> Deploy your final site
Ensure it looks great on both your laptop and your smartphone

reflectionPrompt: Look back at Day 1. What was the single most important concept you mastered this week?
